transcript: if they are sedentary.   Let 's move on to the third pillar.  The third pillar is the pillar of mental health.  And mental health is often neglected. And you know what I realize?  Among the major businesspeople and entrepreneurs, I end up seeing many of them in my office.  She is even more neglected.  People think they're immune, they think they can work too hard, go through a lot of stress, and that it won't be a problem.  For example, among younger men, the main cause of decreased libido in stressed men is not low testosterone, but stress. [music] So, mental health needs to be taken care of, right?  How will a person take care of their mental health?  First, today [music] has a lot of study providing a foundation for meditation.  Meditation is like weight training for the brain. If you don't have time, 5 or 10 minutes in the morning, combine it with something else. I meditate when I go into my sauna, for example.  I combine two things to save time.  So you need to take care of your mental health. If you are feeling anxious or depressed, it's a good idea to seek help, Joel.  And sometimes people feel that they don't need to seek help.  I've been through that before.  In 2017, I was on the verge of burnout for a very, very long time, and no, I am a doctor , I'm not going to medicate myself, I'm going to endure it, I'm a strong guy.  And then suddenly there was a week when I couldn't leave the house.  I woke up, called, canceled the patients, but couldn't go.  Well, at the time I lived alone; my parents, they always lived far away from me.  A week later, my manager at the clinic at the time, [music], she came to my house to see what was going on.  I've been calling for four days and they haven't cancelled appointments.  And there really came a point when, because I thought I was strong, because I thought I was shielded, there came a point when my body said: "Stop." So what I like to tell our patients a lot is, take care of your mental health. Because, nobody is shielded enough that one day it won't tell you that now you can't walk anymore. You were in 2017, right? 2017. Is that what was happening in your life then? In 2017, there were some events happening. First, the VP space was growing more and more. I already had several doctors at the VP space, but I had a very, very high patient appointment rate . I was seeing 17 patients every day, 20 days a month. So I would go into the office at 8 in the morning and, seeing patients and seeing patients, I would leave at 9 or 10 at night. And for me, that was normal. Exactly. In my head, I'm in the construction phase, right? In parallel to that, that year I started opening other clinics. I started opening clinics of  Laser hair removal. So I had the opportunity to get into the business, open a clinic in Balneário Camboriú, and then opened other clinics. Between 2017 and 2021, my partner and I opened 15 clinics. But 2017 was when everything started, and I didn't take my foot off the Volp space and focused on opening the other clinics. So the weekend, which used to be for resting, meant leaving the office on Friday with only the clothes on my back, going to the airport, taking a plane to some city, arriving in that city, and then we'd find the right place, we'd do this, we'd do that. There came a point when my body just stopped, stopped, stopped. I'd wake up and I hadn't even gotten out of bed. I'd think: "No, so [music] why am I doing all this?" So that happened to me. So I like to emphasize this third pillar a lot . And you know what was liberating for me? After some time, I started studying a little bit.  More about mental health, and I saw [music] that has an author named Shawn Archer. Uh-huh. Shawn Archer, he teaches at Harvard about [music] success. And what did he show? He showed that in his research, not only with Harvard students, but he also surveyed presidents of large American companies, and he saw what comes first, happiness or success? And until then, I thought like this : I'm going to run, I'm going to run because I'll reach a certain place where I'll say: "I arrived and I'll be happy." I thought that way. But what he showed and proved [music] with numbers is that it's the opposite, that people who are happy tend to be more successful. [music] Good. And from that moment on, I started allowing myself to do things for myself, for me, for my personal life, not for my business, right? Because until then, everything I did for myself, Joel, I felt guilty. How could I be wasting that precious time? Enjoying myself, enjoying myself.  Imagine, I had to build, I had to do something, I came from a very humble background in Morivia, so I needed to grow, I needed to somehow prove my worth to the world. So every moment I was enjoying was a moment [music] for me that I was missing, until I discovered what I called the happiness advantage. So if you dedicate your energy [music] to being happy, to having good mental health, it greatly increases your chances of being a successful professional. So, if you want to have an impact on the world, start by having an impact on yourself. If you want to help others, start by helping yourself. And the happiness advantage is liberating for those who are workaholics. I don't know if you've ever found yourself [music] in some phases of your life where you suddenly found yourself thinking, "My God, why am I not producing now?" Oh my God.
